data.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection). One affirmation-heavy chunk with one minor coaching finding.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 PASSES):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the proposed dual-run parametrized harness is the canonical "verified fake" mechanism and is data-flow-complete against the actual code. The real client's enrichment surface (`acli_graph.get_comment_map`/`get_parent_map`, `acli.search_issues`) and the existing `FakeAcliClient` (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py) expose IDENTICAL signatures — `get_comment_map(project, jql=None)`, `get_parent_map(project, jql=None)`, `search_issues(jql, start_at, max_results)` — so a single parametrized contract-test body genuinely runs against both. The "fake fails if shape diverges" claim is achievable because the fake returns fixture payloads verbatim with no shape massaging.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the approach solves the NAMED problem (fake-drift reintroducing bug 0ee6's fictional-shape failure), not a proxy. Shape/type assertions (keys + JSON primitive types, not exact values) are the correct anti-change-detector design and directly target the nested-`comment` vs flat-`comments` / absent-`issuelinks` class of bug.
- LIVE GATING: opt-in via the existing `external` marker is already an established codebase convention (tests/external/conftest.py gates on REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL + live Jira creds; test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py / test_reconcile_live.py follow it), so AC4 ("does not flake or slow the default suite") rests on a real, proven seam.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE: the "Why" section states an explicit, well-grounded positive rationale (verified-fake pattern from SWE@Google; prevents bug-0ee6 drift) — G6 item (4) satisfied.
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) a one-directional snapshot/golden-file assertion of the real client's shape (no shared test body) and (b) pure OpenAPI-schema validation of real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) cannot detect fake drift (the named problem) because the fake is never exercised by the same assertions; (b) depends on Jira Cloud spec completeness (an open question the plan itself flags) and still wouldn't pin the FAKE. The chosen shared-contract-body approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on testability and robustness. No coaching finding — alternatives discarded.

COACHING FINDING (minor):
- [ ] Make the "fake fails on divergence" mechanism non-vacuous: state HOW the live-real shape becomes available to the assertion when live is OFF (the default). criteria=[G6]. The harness runs the real client only behind the opt-in live marker, but AC2 ("the fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's") must hold in the DEFAULT (hermetic) suite to actually prevent drift — otherwise drift is only caught on the rare opt-in live run, weakening fitness-for-purpose. Today the fake returns fixtures captured FROM the real client (tests/fixtures/jira/_meta.json: "Scrubbed real Jira REST payloads captured via the production AcliClient methods"), so the same shape-contract test body asserting on the fake's fixture-derived output already exercises real-captured shapes hermetically. The plan should make explicit that the contract assertions run against the fake's fixture output in the default suite (so divergence surfaces hermetically), and the live run is an additional freshness check that the fixtures still match live Jira — not the only place AC2 is enforced. location: "What" bullet 3 ("The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") and AC line 2.
  evidence: ["plan AC2: 'The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.'", "real client only runs behind opt-in live/external marker per 'What' bullet 2 and AC4", "tests/fixtures/jira/_meta.json: fixtures are real captured payloads — the hermetic divergence signal already exists but the plan does not name it as the default-suite enforcement point"]
